If you’re exploring the city and want to find something uniquely San Francisco, the Seward Street Slides are a must-see.

Tucked away in a quiet little park in the Castro neighborhood, these concrete slides have been a local favorite for decades. They’re simple, nostalgic, and surprisingly fun — the kind of place that brings out the kid in everyone.

You’ll see locals, visitors, and families all taking turns sliding down and laughing the whole way. All you need is a small piece of cardboard and you’re ready to go. It’s a quick stop, but it’s one of those quirky San Francisco experiences that makes the city so memorable.

It’s also a great little break if you’re walking around the neighborhood and want to do something different for a few minutes.

A fun, classic San Francisco hidden gem that’s guaranteed to make you smile.
We went two separate days during "open" hours. Everything is locked up. Who controls access to these slides. Kids were super disappointed!
